title: "I've got this…" A Longitudinal Study on the Efficacy of Reducing Test  Anxiety using an AI Mind Palace.
author: PICKERING, GLYN
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This study evaluates the “AI Mind Palace”, a 360° virtual exam environment augmented with AI,  designed to personalise revision and reduce anxiety. Grounded in cognitive theories— including the Method of Loci, context-dependent memory, Bandura’s self-efficacy, and  situational inoculation—the intervention aimed to enhance confidence and preparedness. A  two-year longitudinal mixed-methods study was conducted with 28 post-16 students across  three assessment phases: low-, medium-, and high-stakes exams. Quantitative data from  post-revision surveys and exam results indicated increased confidence and reduced anxiety,  particularly in the high-stakes cohort, where 90% felt more prepared and 88% less anxious. A  strong positive correlation (r ≈ 0.79) was observed between confidence and A-Level  performance. Thematic analysis revealed four key experiences: spatial familiarity,  environmental acclimatisation, anxiety alleviation, and strategic self-regulation. The findings  suggest that immersive, AI-driven environments can effectively support exam readiness and  student wellbeing, particularly in high-pressure contexts.  
   Keywords : test anxiety, virtual reality, Method of Loci, context reinstatement, self-efficacy,  stress inoculation, high-stakes assessment, personalised learning, AI Mind Palace

A  two-year longitudinal mixed-methods study was conducted with 28 post-16 students across  three assessment phases: low-, medium-, and high-stakes exams. Quantitative data from  post-revision surveys and exam results indicated increased confidence and reduced anxiety,  particularly in the high-stakes cohort, where 90% felt more prepared and 88% less anxious. A  strong positive correlation (r ≈ 0.79) was observed between confidence and A-Level  performance. Thematic analysis revealed four key experiences: spatial familiarity,  environmental acclimatisation, anxiety alleviation, and strategic self-regulation. The findings  suggest that immersive, AI-driven environments can effectively support exam readiness and  student wellbeing, particularly in high-pressure contexts.
